Patch Set 2:

> Can you please tell me how a typical HSM release is happening? I saw the file ll_file_hsm_release() is the entry function of HSM release but there is nobody calling it.

This function is called in ll_dir_ioctl() (see dir.c in the patch).
This comes from a llapi call, or "lfs hsm_release FILE1..."

>After some consideration, I think it would be an HSM release open would happen as follows: 1. normal open the file; 2. ioctl to turn it into an exclusive open; 3. do what you want; 4. IT_RELEASE_CLOSE to close the file.

We have to do some specific checks for release before putting file in exclusive open mode. Those checks need to be done in MDT (see mdt_open_by_fid_lock() in the patch).
Does your idea fits with that? It seems this will require a couple of RPC?

By IT_RELEASE_CLOSE, what do you mean by that? What kind of RPC is it? Where do you this intent?

>It turns out IT_RELEASE_OPEN is actually a bad name because exclusive open will be used by multiple modules.

RELEASE_OPEN = EXCLUSIVE OPEN + RELEASE_CHECKS
We can rename it but we need to take care of the release specific checks.

Patch Set 2:

Thank you, Aurelien. In that case, in the implementation of EXCL open, I will support both IT_RELEASE_OPEN and <normal open + ioctl to convert EXCL open> mode.

Patch Set 2:

After this is done, IT_RELEASE_OPEN = EXCL_OPEN + HSM release specific check.

W.r.t IT_RELEASE_CLOSE, I just want to implement it with a separate type of RPC. This is because we not only close file, also do a lot of HSM release specific stuff, so it won't be a good idea to reuse a normal close.

Patch Set 2: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(15 inline comments)

Thank you for the patch.

Patch Set 2:

Hi Aurelien,

Do you like to separate exclusive open implementation from your patch so that I can fix them, if this can make it easier for you.

Patch Set 2:

Jinshan,

As soon as you have something describing your exclusive open implementation, I would like to see it. It could be nice if we could avoid a new IT_RELEASE_OPEN.
Also, I'm not fond of the idea to create a new RPC to manage RELEASE CLOSE as this will require a lot of validation. This new things will need to take care of client code, packing, recovery and a lot of aspect of close code. This will also duplicate of lot of already existing close code.

Patch Set 2:

"Jinshan, As soon as ... close code."

Sure, let me see if I can push a draft patch today.

Patch Set 2:

Hi Aurelien,

I pushed a draft of exclusive open patch to http://review.whamcloud.com/6730, please take a look.

I didn't test the patch yet, but it will be pretty much implemented this way.

Jinshan

Patch Set 5:

Hi Aurelien,

I reimplemented this patch based in exclusive open, so you can see this patch is now dependent on LU-2919. I did the following thing to make it work:
1. Get rid of IT_RELEASE_OPEN, now release open is represented by IT_OPEN + MDS_OPEN_RELEASE;
2. Get rid of busy file detection on the MDT side to use exclusive open;
3. OBD_MD_FLRELEASED flag is introduced, this flag will be set if release close is run successfully on the MDT. Clients need to check this flag to know if release is done.

Can you please ran this patch to make sure everything is okay?
Uploaded patch set 6.

parents.subject: LU-2919 hsm: Implementation of exclusive open

commit.subject: LU-1333 hsm: Add hsm_release feature.
commit.message:
LU-1333 hsm: Add hsm_release feature.

HSM Release is one of the key feature of HSM. It removes file
LOV objects, if the file has been archived previously.

This patch is implementing all mechanisms, from client to MDS,
to do a release, modifying LOV EA, triggering actions and checking
the file is in a correct state.

Signed-off-by: Aurelien Degremont <aurelien.degremont@cea.fr>
Signed-off-by: Jinshan Xiong <jinshan.xiong@intel.com>
Change-Id: If910351cf1195551ab6f65535d09a1ea984ec3f3
